reasoning: I will extract and present the prevention recommendations for neonatal conjunctivitis from the provided text as concise bullet points.
output:
- Pregnant women should receive treatment for sexually transmitted infections (such as gonorrhea and chlamydia) to prevent newborn conjunctivitis caused by these infections.
- Prophylactic eye drops (eye drops with antibiotics) are placed in all infants' eyes in the delivery room right after birth to help prevent many infections; most states have laws requiring this treatment.
- If the mother has active herpes sores at the time of delivery, a Cesarean section (C-section) is recommended to prevent serious illness in the baby.
